L1.area: Template rendering and output encoding layer

L2.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in a template rendering engine where autoescape is explicitly disabled by default. User-supplied data rendered through templates is not HTML-escaped, allowing the injection and execution of malicious scripts in users' browsers when visiting affected web pages.

L3.area: Django-compatible template rendering engine and its initialization configuration
L3.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the Django-compatible template rendering engine where autoescape is explicitly disabled by default during template set initialization. User-supplied data rendered through this template engine is not HTML-escaped, allowing the injection and execution of malicious scripts in users' browsers.

ground_truth.reason: The Django template engine for Fiber explicitly disables autoescape by default, causing user-supplied data to be rendered without HTML escaping, enabling cross-site scripting attacks.
